title: HUD, Text, Menus, Focus, Multichannel Cues, and Motion Settings · Contract Workshop
summary: A two-person studio incrementally builds accessible responsive game interface and settings flow from original geometric fixtures while retaining earlier Python, repository, determinism, frame-budget, accessibility, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of accessible responsive game interface and settings flow while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging…
objectives: Choose scalable fonts, line spacing, contrast, alignment, safe areas, localization space, and structured labels for essential text.
Support predictable keyboard and controller navigation, wrap policy, back, confirmation, cancellation, and no traps.
Render persistent non-color focus and announce or retain important state, errors, destructive actions, and setting changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Readable Text
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Asteroids team handles case 5922 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to build readable keyboard and controller menus, HUD, focus, status, multi-channel cues, text scaling, and reduced-motion options; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Readable Text.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Readable Text has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Plan the evidence sequence · Workshop Readable Text, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Readable Text precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Plan the evidence sequence · Workshop Readable Text, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A workshop Asteroids team handles case 5208 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to build readable keyboard and controller menus, HUD, focus, status, multi-channel cues, text scaling, and reduced-motion options; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose scalable fonts, line spacing, contrast, alignment, safe areas, localization space, and structured labels for essential text.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Plan the evidence sequence · Workshop Readable Text, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: High resolution makes any font size readable.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Motion Photosensitivity
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-motion-photosensitivity-wg7lk3" that uses case 3414 to build readable keyboard and controller menus, HUD, focus, status, multi-channel cues, text scaling, and reduced-motion options. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Motion Photosensitivity.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Motion Photosensitivity into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Motion Photosensitivity, keep Motion Photosensitivity connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-motion-photosensitivity-wg7lk3
# Competency: asteroids-motion-photosensitivity.
# Case 3414: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def evidence_asteroids_motion_photosensitivity_wg7lk3(critical_signals: int = 8, multi_channel_signals: int = 8, focusable_controls: int = 7, focused_controls: int = 7, reduced_motion: bool = True, scalable_text: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_wg7lk3 = "wg7lk3-3kl7gw-3414"
    assert evidence_variant_wg7lk3
    evidence_axes_wg7lk3 = (
        "playerwg7lk3",
        "seed3414",
        "input3kl7gw",
        "framewg7lk33414",
        "state3414wg7lk3",
        "playtest3kl7gw3414",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_wg7lk3)) == 6
    if critical_signals < 1 or focusable_controls < 1:
        return False, critical_signals, "ui-domain"
    if multi_channel_signals != critical_signals:
        return False, multi_channel_signals, "color-only-signal"
    if focused_controls != focusable_controls:
        return False, focused_controls, "focus-gap"
    if not reduced_motion or not scalable_text:
        return False, focusable_controls, "display-access"
    assert focused_controls == focusable_controls
    return True, focused_controls, "accessible-game-ui"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Motion Photosensitivity structural check is only one gate. accessible responsive game interface and settings flow must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Motion Photosensitivity, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Motion Photosensitivity precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Motion Photosensitivity, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A workshop Asteroids team handles case 3589 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to build readable keyboard and controller menus, HUD, focus, status, multi-channel cues, text scaling, and reduced-motion options; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Offer reduced motion and effects, avoid unsafe flashes, cap screen shake, allow pause, and preserve essential alternatives.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Motion Photosensitivity, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Retro flashing is harmless when individual flashes are short.

steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Asteroids team handles case 1970 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Offer reduced motion and effects, avoid unsafe flashes, cap screen shake, allow pause, and preserve essential alternatives.

stimulus.title: Motion Photosensitivity failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Retro flashing is harmless when individual flashes are short.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Offer reduced motion and effects, avoid unsafe flashes, cap screen shake, allow pause, and preserve essential alternatives.

options.text: Keep the plausible Motion Photosensitivity output and hide the violated accessible responsive game interface and settings flow assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Motion Photosensitivity.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Motion Photosensitivity for accessible responsive game interface and settings flow.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Motion Photosensitivity condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Offer reduced motion and effects, avoid unsafe flashes, cap screen shake, allow pause, and preserve essential alternatives.
